  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "New middleware implementation adds context-based logging with potential type assertion failures and incomplete error handling in the logger wrapping layer.",

    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Defensive Programming / Type Safety",
      "file": "pkg/plugins/log/logger.go",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "Unchecked type assertion in FromContext may lose logger state",
      "description": "In the FromContext method, the code attempts to cast d.l.FromContext(ctx) to *log.ConcreteLogger with an ok check, but falls back to d.New() on type mismatch. This fallback loses any contextual attributes that were set in the original logger context, potentially breaking traceability when the underlying logger type doesn't match expectations. The FromContext should either preserve the original logger or properly propagate context attributes.",
      "suggestion": "Consider wrapping the result in grafanaInfraLogWrapper regardless of type, or ensure the fallback d.New() call preserves context attributes from ctx using log.WithContextualAttributes if needed."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Incomplete Refactoring",
      "file": "pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/logger_middleware.go",
      "line": null,
      "title": "logRequest signature changed but callers may not be updated",
      "description": "The logRequest method signature was changed from accepting pluginCtx and endpoint parameters to removing them. The diff shows these were removed from the function signature, but the actual call sites in QueryData, CallResource, etc. are not visible in the diff. If callers still pass these parameters, this will cause compilation errors. If they've been updated to not pass them, the endpoint and pluginId logging context has been moved to ContextualLoggerMiddleware, but this refactoring is not verifiable from the visible diff.",
      "suggestion": "Verify that all call sites to logRequest have been updated to match the new signature and that plugin context logging is properly handled by ContextualLoggerMiddleware."
    }
  ]
}
